channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🦠🌍 WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak Global Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization declared an Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern. The rare Bundibugyo strain has no approved vaccine and has killed nearly 90 people.

item.title: 🇮🇱🏛️ Israel to Build Defense Offices in Former UNRWA Jerusalem Compound
item.description: Israel cabinet approved building a defense compound on the site of recently demolished UNRWA premises in East Jerusalem. The UN had called the demolition a violation of international law.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Wave of Ukrainian Drone Strikes Kills Four in Russia
item.description: Ukraine launched its largest drone attack on Moscow in over a year, with over 550 drones intercepted across a dozen Russian regions. Four people were killed including three in the Moscow region.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Republicans Vie to Replace Defeated Senator Cassidy in Louisiana
item.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy was defeated in the primary after his impeachment vote against Trump. Representative Julia Letlow and State Treasurer John Fleming advance to the runoff.

item.title: ✈️🚨 Air Force Base Locked Down After Idaho Air Show Incident
item.description: Emergency crews responded to an aircraft incident during the Gunfighter Skies Air Show at Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho. Witnesses reported mid-air collision involving Navy jets with parachutes seen descending.

item.title: 🇨🇺⚔️ Cuba seeking Russian drones, military equipment as US warns of Guantanamo threat
item.description: US intelligence reports Cuba has acquired over 300 Russian and Iranian drones and is discussing attacks on Guantanamo Bay, Key West, and US military vessels. Cuban officials are studying how Iran has resisted US military pressure.

item.title: 🇺🇦💥 Ukraine drone strike hits Russian fertilizer plant linked to explosives production
item.description: A fire broke out at Nevinnomyssk Azot plant in Stavropol Krai following a drone strike. The facility is one of Russia's largest mineral fertilizer producers and a critical component of Russia's defense-industrial complex for explosives manufacturing.

item.title: 🛢️🇺🇸 US sanctions waiver for Russian oil purchases expires without renewal
item.description: The Treasury Department allowed the waiver permitting countries to purchase Russian oil cargoes stranded at sea to expire on May 16. Treasury Secretary Bessent had previously said there were no plans to renew the authorization.

item.title: 🇫🇷⚖️ New Epstein victims emerge in French investigation
item.description: Paris prosecutor says around 10 new victims have come forward since France launched a special probe into Jeffrey Epstein's network in February. Investigators are working to interview victims located abroad.

item.title: 🇨🇺 US Intelligence Warns of Cuban Drone Threat to Guantanamo Bay
item.description: Classified intelligence reports Cuba has acquired over 300 military drones and discussed plans to attack the US base at Guantanamo Bay, US naval vessels, and potentially Key West, Florida.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President Rejects Being "Sacrificed," Defends US Arms Sales
item.description: President Lai Ching-te said Taiwan will not be traded away and US arms sales represent a security commitment, responding to Trump comments urging both sides to cool down.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Economy Contracts in Q1 as Iran War Takes Toll
item.description: Israels economy shrank at an annualized 3.3% in the first quarter, hit by the war with Iran, but growth is expected to recover if the conflict does not reignite.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 China Commits to B Annual US Agricultural Purchases Through 2028
item.description: China has agreed to purchase at least billion of US agricultural products annually through 2028, the White House announced, offering stability to American farmers after Phase One trade deal.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump to Hold Situation Room Meeting Tuesday on Iran Military Options
item.description: President Trump is expected to meet with top national security advisers on Tuesday to discuss military options regarding Iran, as ceasefire negotiations remain stalled.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Commander Vows Relentless Strikes Into Russia
item.description: Ukrainian unmanned forces commander "Madyar" says Kyiv will continue targeting Russian defense industry and oil facilities across all of Russia, from the Urals to Siberia, to deprive Moscow of war funding. The statement came before Ukraine launched nearly 600 drones over Russia.

item.title: 🇮🇱 IDF Reports Shortage of 12,000 Troops Amid Multi-Front War
item.description: The Israeli military announced a critical manpower gap of approximately 12,000 soldiers, including 6,000-7,500 combat troops, as it operates across seven different arenas. Officials warn the shortage will worsen if mandatory service is reduced to 30 months.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Kremlin Signals Interest in Restoring Dialogue With Europe
item.description: Russian spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said Moscow could restart dialogue with Europe over the war in Ukraine, speaking after the large-scale Ukrainian drone attack on Moscow. The remarks come as European officials debate future engagement with Russia.

item.title: 🇹🇭 Train crash kills 8 in Bangkok, driver charged with negligence
item.description: A freight train collided with a public bus at a rail crossing in central Bangkok, killing eight people and injuring 32. Thai police have charged the train driver with negligence as investigations continue.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Islamist attack on Nigerian military school kills 17 police trainees
item.description: Suspected Islamist militants attacked a specialized military school in Yobe state, killing at least 17 police officers in training. The attack marks a major escalation in northeast Nigeria's ongoing insurgency.

item.title: 🇺🇸 New York's largest commuter rail system shuts down in first strike since 1994
item.description: The Long Island Rail Road ceased operations as five unions walked off the job after months of stalled contract negotiations. Governor Hochul pleaded with unions to resume talks before Monday's rush hour, as 250,000 daily commuters face disruption.

item.title: 🇵🇪 Peru electoral body pledges to fix voting flaws before June presidential runoff
item.description: Peru's top electoral authority acknowledged flaws that delayed April's first-round results by a month. The runoff between Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez is scheduled for June 7, following weeks of political uncertainty.

item.title: 🇺🇸 USS Ford Aircraft Carrier Returns Home After 11-Month Iran War Deployment
item.description: The USS Ford aircraft carrier has returned to its home port after an 11-month deployment that supported operations related to the Iran conflict and the capture of Venezuelan leader Maduro. The carrier was one of the longest deployments for a US aircraft carrier in recent years.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Insists It Is Independent After Trump Warning From Beijing Summit
item.description: Taiwan has reaffirmed its independent status after President Trump, fresh from his summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing, warned against a formal declaration of independence by the island. The statement comes amid heightened US-China tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇹 Car Rams Into Pedestrians in Italy, Injuring Eight People
item.description: A driver plowed into pedestrians in Italy, injuring eight people, four seriously. Passers-by chased and stopped the attacker. The incident is being investigated for possible terrorist motives.

item.title: 🇳🇬 More Than 50 Schoolchildren Including Toddlers Kidnapped in Nigeria
item.description: Armed gunmen have kidnapped more than 50 schoolchildren, including toddlers, in an attack on a school in Borno state, Nigeria. No group has claimed responsibility for the attack, which has devastated the local community.
